serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of black bean soup is one bowl, approximately 300 grams, providing around 220 kcal per serving.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Black bean soup, home-prepared (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from the input record, which was originally sourced from GrabFood menu data citing HPB Singapore for menu listing only. Core macro and micronutrient values are cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central entries for black bean soup (FDC ID 173735) and are consistent with published values for a home-prepared black bean soup. HPB Singapore Foodo does not carry a direct entry for this dish. USDA SR Legacy is used as the approved secondary global source.

gi_source.title: Black beans, boiled — Glycemic Index entry
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI for black bean soup is estimated from the GI of boiled black beans (GI ~30) as listed in the University of Sydney GI Database, since black beans are the dominant carbohydrate source. The high dietary fibre content of the soup (5.0g/100g) further supports a low GI classification. GL per serving calculated as: (30 × 12.67g carbohydrate per 100g × 300g serving) / 100 = 11.4g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 216.7mg/100g partially offset by high potassium (~320mg/100g) and dietary fibre
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should use low-sodium vegetable broth and avoid adding table salt to keep sodium below 200mg/100g, while benefiting from the soup's potassium and fibre content which are DASH-diet-aligned blood pressure reducers.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~30), high dietary fibre (5.0g/100g), and moderate protein (4.0g/100g) collectively moderate postprandial glucose response
type2_diabetes.guidance: Black bean soup is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its low GI (~30), high dietary fibre, and plant-based protein content; portion control to one bowl (300g) is advised to manage cumulative carbohydrate load of approximately 38g per serving.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low saturated fat (0.17g/100g), zero cholesterol, high dietary fibre, and plant-based protein
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Black bean soup supports cardiovascular health through its negligible saturated fat, zero cholesterol, and high soluble fibre content which can help reduce LDL cholesterol; prepare with minimal added oil and low-sodium broth for optimal benefit.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory spices, high fibre supporting gut microbiome, and negligible saturated fat
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The soup's high fibre content supports gut microbiome immune regulation and anti-inflammatory spices such as cumin, garlic, and oregano provide polyphenol benefits; individuals sensitive to nightshades (bell peppers, tomatoes) should monitor their individual response.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Black Bean Soup is a hearty, thick, and deeply flavourful Mexican-style soup made from tender slow-simmered black beans (also known as turtle beans or frijoles negros) combined with aromatic vegetables including onion, garlic, bell pepper, and tomato, and seasoned with warm spices such as cumin, oregano, and coriander. The soup has a rich, earthy, and slightly smoky taste with a creamy, velvety texture from the partially mashed beans. It is typically prepared by sautéing aromatics, adding dried or pre-soaked black beans with vegetable broth, and simmering for 45–90 minutes until the beans are fully tender. Culturally, black bean soup is a staple of Mexican and broader Latin American cuisine, and has been adopted widely across Southeast Asia including Singapore as a nutritious plant-based meal option. Clinically, it is notable for its low glycemic index (~30), high dietary fibre (5.0g/100g), moderate plant-based protein (4.0g/100g), negligible saturated fat (0.17g/100g), and zero cholesterol, making it suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, and hypertension. The high potassium content (~320mg/100g) further supports blood pressure regulation. It is naturally vegetarian, vegan, and gluten-free, and is an excellent source of iron (1.33mg/100g) and calcium (26.67mg/100g) for a plant-based dish.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values from the input record are consistent with USDA FoodData Central data for black bean soup and are plausible for a home-prepared Mexican-style black bean soup. The original input cited HPB Singapore for menu listing only, not for nutrient data. USDA SR Legacy is used as the approved cross-reference. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using boiled black beans as the reference food. Confidence is medium rather than high because no exact approved-source entry for this specific dish preparation was directly matched.

Trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero — plant-based dish with no hydrogenated fats). Cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (naturally zero — entirely plant-based preparation). FoodEx2 code A00ML (Soups and broths) is used as the closest defensible parent category; no specific FoodEx2 leaf code for black bean soup was identified in the FoodEx2 R3 hierarchy.
